name: sig-001-known-key-known-said
description: Positive: sign a known envelope SAID with a known Ed25519 signing key, expect a deterministic signature. Ed25519 (RFC 8032) is deterministic, so the signature is reproducible across implementations.

name: sig-002-verify-positive
description: Positive: verify a signature against the envelope SAID and verifying key it was produced for. Result MUST be true.

name: sig-003-tampered-envelope
description: Negative: verify a signature against a *different* envelope SAID than the one it was produced for. The signature is structurally valid but binds a different SAID; verification MUST return false. This catches the most common attack: an adversary swaps the envelope and forwards the original signature.

name: sig-004-wrong-key
description: Negative: verify a signature against the SAID it was produced for, but with a *different* verifying key. Verification MUST return false. This catches the case where an adversary supplies a valid signature from a key that is not the byte's current owner.

name: sig-005-malformed-signature
description: Negative: verify a structurally malformed signature (wrong length, in this case 63 bytes instead of 64). Verification MUST return false and the implementation MUST NOT panic or leak timing information distinguishing this case from the cryptographic-failure case.
